channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: There are a number of potholes in Windsor street, a number of then, 16th May
item.description:
Windsor street is narrow and therefore traffic from both directions overlap. Potholes are visibly deepening by the day. Residents in the nursing home must be being disturbed.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 56, Windsor Street, Lynworth, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Motorbikes misusing restricted byway, 16th May
item.description:
1.30 pm 16/05/2026. 5-6 motorbikes illegally used this restricted byway, clearly marked as per photo. They accessed this path by cutting a corner through farmer’s field to the east, to avoid the difficult to open gate at the entrance to this byway. Not only is this illegal, but very dangerous as it’s a very narrow footpath used by walkers and horses.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elkstone, Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Branchs, 16th May
item.description:
Branches are breaking off and falling on private drive. There is a large branch in tree looks dead and is at risk of falling and damaging the property of 228 Brooklyn Road Please could you address this as a matter of Urgency.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 214, Brooklyn Road, Kingsditch, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Volunteer litter pick bags ready for collection, 16th May
item.description:
Nexr to the Innsworth Lane Sports Ground sign. The 'What 3 Words' location is ///examiner.slopes.courage The ordnance survey map reference is: 51.883919,-2.213313 Council Litter Bin Ref: 0254

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Innsworth Lane, Longlevens, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Kerb damaged sharp edge damaged tyre, 16th May
item.description:
The kerb has been damaged with a large chunk missing. I’ve damaged my tyre on it needing to spend £140 on a replacement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Alstone Croft, Marle Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road is really difficult to cycle and drive, 15th May
item.description:
Very bumpy. Lots of potholes and dips which makes driving on it very uncomfortable. Cycling it is dangerous to ones health!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 34, Grafton Road, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: URGENT: Roadworks barriers illegally removed, traffic driving under dangerous hanging tree — Ref: EY102GH19010000041919-01, 15th May
item.description:
Members of the public have illegally removed the road closure barriers adjacent to the A40 and left them to one side. This closure was actively in place for public safety due to an unsafe, hanging tree (Works Reference: EY102GH19010000041919-01), scheduled from 15th May to 19th May. Because the barriers have been displaced, vehicles are now driving directly under the dangerous tree. Please reinstate the closure urgently to prevent a serious accident. To ensure these barriers are not removed illegally again, is it possible to deploy a more secure, heavy-duty, or locked traffic management solution rather than standard plastic barriers.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Whittington Court Lane, Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Signs & Barriers left behind by Contractor
